subSkills.name: using-superpowers
subSkills.description: Use when starting any conversation — establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions.

subSkills.name: brainstorming
subSkills.description: Use before any creative work — creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

subSkills.name: writing-plans
subSkills.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code.

subSkills.name: executing-plans
subSkills.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints.

subSkills.name: writing-skills
subSkills.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment.

subSkills.name: subagent-driven-development
subSkills.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session.

subSkills.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
subSkills.description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies.

subSkills.name: using-git-worktrees
subSkills.description: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans — ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback.

subSkills.name: test-driven-development
subSkills.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code.

subSkills.name: systematic-debugging
subSkills.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes.

subSkills.name: requesting-code-review
subSkills.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements.

subSkills.name: receiving-code-review
subSkills.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable — requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation.

subSkills.name: verification-before-completion
subSkills.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs — requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always.

subSkills.name: finishing-a-development-branch
subSkills.description: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work — guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup.
